title: Project Management Office - PMO
required_skills: Zusatzqualifikationen im Projektmanagement (e.g. PMP, PRINCE2, IPMA)
Fundierte Erfahrung im Programm-/Projektmanagement komplexer technischer Vorhaben
Erfahrung im Angebots-/Proposal-Management ODER Management großer Multi-Stakeholder-Programme
matched_skills: Programme governance & PMO operations (HSBC USD 8M programme, 6 workstreams)
C-suite / executive status reporting and data storytelling
Multi-stakeholder coordination across Product, Engineering, Marketing, C-suite
missing_skills: Technical/engineering domain PMO experience (Aerospace, Defense, or industrial engineering context implied by Bremen + ALTEN client base)
Formal PM certification (PMP, PRINCE2, or IPMA) — Power BI cert (PL-300) does not qualify as Projektmanagement Zusatzqualifikation
Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) planning — not explicitly evidenced in CV
Proposal / bid management (Angebotsprozess, time-cost structure for engineering tenders)
Milestone and dependency management in large-scale technical programmes
Escalation template creation and steering committee facilitation
Collaboration with Systems Architect or Requirements & Interface Manager roles
core_responsibilities: Build and maintain programme planning including WBS, milestones, dependencies, and resources
Own risk and opportunity management including measure tracking and escalation template creation
Steer steering meetings and status reporting; define escalation logic and decision frameworks
Collaborate closely with Systems Architect, Commercial & Subcontract Manager, and Requirements & Interface Manager
Support the proposal phase covering time/cost structure, assumptions, bid process, and deliverable planning
recommended_emphasis: Lead with HSBC USD 8M programme governance: 6 workstreams, C-suite escalation, risk prioritisation — this is the strongest direct match to Steering/Status-Reporting responsibilities
Quantify status reporting cadence at HSBC (weekly burn-rate reports, executive reviews) to mirror the steering committee reporting requirement
Reframe Hapag-Lloyd sprint coordination using programme planning language: dependency tracking, milestone ownership, cross-team delivery
Explicitly surface any WBS or structured scheduling experience, even if informal, since the JD uses this terminology directly
If a formal PM certification (PMP, PRINCE2, IPMA) is achievable short-term, flag it; otherwise emphasise structured governance methodology as a substitute signal
Neutralise the FinTech/digital framing where possible and use domain-agnostic programme management language to reduce perceived domain mismatch
Highlight B1 German and willingness to work in Bremen as practical fit signals for this location-specific role
score_rationale: Candidate has solid PMO foundations — governance, risk escalation, C-level reporting, multi-stakeholder coordination — that align with the role's operational core. Primary deductions: (1) role targets engineering/technical PMO in an industrial or Aerospace/Defense context; candidate has no engineering domain background; (2) WBS and formal proposal/bid management are not evidenced in the CV; (3) no formal PM certification (PMP/PRINCE2/IPMA) — Power BI cert does not satisfy Zusatzqualifikationen im Projektmanagement. Score 52 reflects meaningful process-level overlap but a significant domain depth gap.
